title: Label 309 passphrase block
description: The passphrase-derivation block of a sealed-PoE envelope: a CLOSED CBOR map `{alg, salt, params}` describing how the content-encryption key is derived from a shared passphrase (instead of, and mutually exclusive with, per-recipient KEM slots). `alg` is the KDF identifier (open string; registry membership ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED is a domain-pass check). `salt` is a 16..64-byte byte string (hex; below/above the bound emits ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_SHORT / ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_LONG). `params` is the CLOSED map `{m, t, p}` of CBOR unsigned integers, exactly as the grammar pins it for the registered alg (argon2id); extra keys are rejected (SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD). The memory/time/parallelism FLOORS (m >= 65536, t >= 3, p >= 1) and the deployment ceilings are domain-pass checks (ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW / ENC_PASSPHRASE_PARAMS_EXCEED_POLICY), not asserted structurally. There is no on-chain MAC or verifier field on the passphrase path: the key commitment lives inside the ciphertext blob.

salt.$comment: 16..64 bytes, modelled as a 32..128-character lowercase-hex string (each byte = 2 hex chars).

params.title: KDF parameters
params.description: Closed map of CBOR unsigned integers (0 .. 2^32 − 1, exact-integer handling): `m` (memory cost, KiB), `t` (passes), `p` (parallelism).
